description: The Avenger Street 160 is Bajaj's answer to riders who want a cruiser without the cruiser price tag. Low seat, forward-set pegs, and a relaxed riding position make it comfortable for long highway stretches. It will not win any drag races, but it gets you there in style and comfort.
highlights: Low seat height for all riders
pros: Relaxed cruiser position reduces fatigue on long rides
Low seat height works for riders of all sizes
Highway stability is surprisingly good for the price
Looks like a much more expensive bike
cons: Heavy steering in tight city traffic
Ground clearance is low — speed bumps are a challenge

common_problems.solution: Allow adequate warm-up, avoid prolonged idling
Re-lubricate chain after riding in rain

avoid: Low-quality seat covers (trap heat, cause discomfort)
buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories fit perfectly but cost more. For the Avenger Street 160, aftermarket offers good value for non-critical items like phone mounts and bike covers. For crash protection, go OEM or from a trusted brand.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save money
Skipping crash guard — one tip-over can cost thousands in repairs
Using WD-40 as chain lube — it is a cleaner, not a lubricant
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
